title: Product Note — Smart Amount Suggestions
subtitle: Product: The Dynamic Amount‑Suggestion feature automatically shows The Dynamic Amount‑Suggestion feature automatically s

document_meta.recipient_line: All Member Banks, PSP Banks and UPI Applications
document_meta.subject_line: Subject: Product: The Dynamic Amount‑Suggestion feature automatically shows ## Feature The **Dynamic Amount‑Suggestion** feature automatically shows
document_meta.desired_outcome: Complete document suite for Smart Amount Suggestions

sections.heading: Document Overview
sections.content_instructions:
Write purpose, audience, and scope as three short sub-sections.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Current State: Today a UPI payment follows a multi‑step flow where the payer app sends a ReqPay to its PSP, NPCI obtains authentication from the payee PSP via ReqAuthDetail, the payer PSP then sends a debit request (ReqDebit) to NPCI, and finally credit is posted to the merchant through ReqCredit. Amount entry is manual and the app validates PIN only when the amount exceeds the configured threshold.
Limitations / Pain Points: Manual amount entry leads to slower checkout and higher typo‑related failures. PSPs and merchants have no visibility into the user's historical payment patterns, missing an opportunity to streamline repeat payments. The current flow also does not provide a UI mechanism for one‑tap selection of frequently used amounts.
RULE: Stakeholder-friendly language only. No XML, no internal class names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required]** We assume the payer will be identified by the standard UPI Virtual Payment Address (VPA) – the same identifier used in the existing ReqPay message. Confirm, or specify a different identifier type (e.g., mobile number, Aadhaar‑linked ID).
A: yes proceed

Q: **[Required]** We assume the payee will also be identified by a VPA, matching the current payeeVPA field in the ReqPay request. Confirm, or indicate if an alternative identifier (e.g., merchant ID, phone number) is required.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** For the smart‑suggested‑amount flow we will apply the same per‑transaction ceiling as the baseline UPI rule – INR 15,000 – with mandatory PIN enforcement above that limit. Confirm, or provide a different maximum amount that should apply specifically to suggested‑amount payments.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** We will inherit the payer’s existing daily UPI aggregate limit set by the PSP (e.g., ₹1 lakh per day) for transactions generated via the suggestion UI. Confirm, or specify a distinct daily cap that should be enforced for this feature.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** To deliver the amount suggestions we propose adding two new API messages:  
   * **SuggestionRequest** – sent from the payer PSP to NPCI (or a designated suggestion service) with payerVPA and payeeVPA to retrieve historic amounts.  
   * **SuggestionResponse** – returns a list (up to three) of suggested amounts plus optional metadata (frequency, recency).  

   The existing ReqPay flow will remain unchanged after the user selects a suggestion. Confirm, or outline any additional request/response fields or alternative message names you prefer.
A: yes
sections.prompt_instruction: Write Purpose, Audience, and Scope sub-sections in short prose. No bullets unless needed for stakeholder names.

sections.prompt_instruction: Use prose for current state and concise bullets for limitations and benefits.

sections.heading: Product Overview - Feature Description
sections.content_instructions:
Explain what the feature is, what it enables, and the security/privacy principles in plain language.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Current State: Today a UPI payment follows a multi‑step flow where the payer app sends a ReqPay to its PSP, NPCI obtains authentication from the payee PSP via ReqAuthDetail, the payer PSP then sends a debit request (ReqDebit) to NPCI, and finally credit is posted to the merchant through ReqCredit. Amount entry is manual and the app validates PIN only when the amount exceeds the configured threshold.
Limitations / Pain Points: Manual amount entry leads to slower checkout and higher typo‑related failures. PSPs and merchants have no visibility into the user's historical payment patterns, missing an opportunity to streamline repeat payments. The current flow also does not provide a UI mechanism for one‑tap selection of frequently used amounts.
Functional Requirements:
  FR-01: The system shall retrieve the payer's last N transaction amounts for a given payee from the NPCI transaction history.
  FR-02: The system shall display up to three suggested amounts on the payment screen, ranked by recency and frequency.
  FR-03: The payer shall be able to select a suggested amount with a single tap.
  FR-04: The selected amount shall be editable before final confirmation, preserving the one‑tap convenience.
  FR-05: The payment request generated from a suggested amount shall follow the existing ReqPay → ReqAuthDetail → ReqDebit flow unchanged.
  FR-06: For suggested amounts >15,000, the UI shall enforce mandatory UPI PIN entry as per existing PIN rules.
RULE: Stakeholder-friendly language only. No XML, no internal class names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required]** We assume the payer will be identified by the standard UPI Virtual Payment Address (VPA) – the same identifier used in the existing ReqPay message. Confirm, or specify a different identifier type (e.g., mobile number, Aadhaar‑linked ID).
A: yes proceed

Q: **[Required]** We assume the payee will also be identified by a VPA, matching the current payeeVPA field in the ReqPay request. Confirm, or indicate if an alternative identifier (e.g., merchant ID, phone number) is required.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** For the smart‑suggested‑amount flow we will apply the same per‑transaction ceiling as the baseline UPI rule – INR 15,000 – with mandatory PIN enforcement above that limit. Confirm, or provide a different maximum amount that should apply specifically to suggested‑amount payments.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** We will inherit the payer’s existing daily UPI aggregate limit set by the PSP (e.g., ₹1 lakh per day) for transactions generated via the suggestion UI. Confirm, or specify a distinct daily cap that should be enforced for this feature.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** To deliver the amount suggestions we propose adding two new API messages:  
   * **SuggestionRequest** – sent from the payer PSP to NPCI (or a designated suggestion service) with payerVPA and payeeVPA to retrieve historic amounts.  
   * **SuggestionResponse** – returns a list (up to three) of suggested amounts plus optional metadata (frequency, recency).  

   The existing ReqPay flow will remain unchanged after the user selects a suggestion. Confirm, or outline any additional request/response fields or alternative message names you prefer.
A: yes

sections.heading: Product Construct: Setting / Enrollment
sections.content_instructions:
If the feature requires explicit enrollment or opt-in by the user: describe UI placement, step-by-step enrollment journey, consent capture, and participant roles. If the feature does NOT require a separate enrollment or setting step (e.g., it is auto-enabled, accessed directly from the payment screen, or triggered from recent transactions): write exactly two sentences stating that this section is not applicable for this feature and briefly explain why (e.g., 'This feature does not require a separate enrollment step. Users access it directly from [describe entry point] without any prior registration or opt-in.'). NEVER leave this section blank.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
APIs: [
  {
    "name": "ReqPay",
    "response": "RespPay",
    "initiator": "Payer PSP",
    "description": "Initiates a payment request with type Pay and includes payer/payee details."
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqAuthDetail",
    "response": "RespAuthDetail",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Requests authentication details from the Payee PSP for the initiated payment."
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqTxnConfirmation",
    "response": "RespTxnConfirmation",
    "initiator": "Payer PSP",
    "description": "Confirms transaction status after debit/credit processing."
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqDebit",
    "response": "RespDebit",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Requests debit against the payer's account or voucher as part of the payment flow."
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqCredit",
    "response": "RespCredit",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Requests credit to the merchant/beneficiary account after successful debit."
  }
]
Flow Sequence:
  Step 1: Payer PSP — initiates ReqPay with type Pay, amount, payerVPA, payeeVPA.
  Step 2: NPCI — sends ReqAuthDetail to Payee PSP for authentication.
  Step 3: Payee PSP — validates merchant details, returns RespAuthDetail with AC/IFSC.
  Step 4: NPCI — forwards ReqDebit to payer's account/voucher based on amount and purposeCode.
  Step 5: Payer PSP — sends ReqTxnConfirmation to NPCI after debit.
  Step 6: NPCI — triggers ReqCredit to Payee PSP/merchant.
  Step 7: Payee PSP — sends RespTxnConfirmation to NPCI confirming credit.
Auth Method: UPI PIN
Transaction Limit: INR 15,000 per transaction for standard flow; amounts >15,000 require UPI PIN entry.
User Journey:
  Step 1: User opens the UPI app and selects a contact or merchant.
  Step 2: PSP app fetches recent transaction amounts for that payee from NPCI and shows up to three suggested amounts.
  Step 3: User taps a suggested amount; the amount field is pre‑filled and remains editable.
  Step 4: If amount >15,000, the app prompts for UPI PIN; otherwise proceeds.
  Step 5: PSP sends ReqPay (with chosen amount) to NPCI.
  Step 6: NPCI performs authentication (ReqAuthDetail/RespAuthDetail) and processes debit/credit as per existing flow.
  Step 7: User receives transaction confirmation (RespTxnConfirmation).
RULE: Stakeholder-friendly language only. No XML, no internal class names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required]** We assume the payer will be identified by the standard UPI Virtual Payment Address (VPA) – the same identifier used in the existing ReqPay message. Confirm, or specify a different identifier type (e.g., mobile number, Aadhaar‑linked ID).
A: yes proceed

Q: **[Required]** We assume the payee will also be identified by a VPA, matching the current payeeVPA field in the ReqPay request. Confirm, or indicate if an alternative identifier (e.g., merchant ID, phone number) is required.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** For the smart‑suggested‑amount flow we will apply the same per‑transaction ceiling as the baseline UPI rule – INR 15,000 – with mandatory PIN enforcement above that limit. Confirm, or provide a different maximum amount that should apply specifically to suggested‑amount payments.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** We will inherit the payer’s existing daily UPI aggregate limit set by the PSP (e.g., ₹1 lakh per day) for transactions generated via the suggestion UI. Confirm, or specify a distinct daily cap that should be enforced for this feature.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** To deliver the amount suggestions we propose adding two new API messages:  
   * **SuggestionRequest** – sent from the payer PSP to NPCI (or a designated suggestion service) with payerVPA and payeeVPA to retrieve historic amounts.  
   * **SuggestionResponse** – returns a list (up to three) of suggested amounts plus optional metadata (frequency, recency).  

   The existing ReqPay flow will remain unchanged after the user selects a suggestion. Confirm, or outline any additional request/response fields or alternative message names you prefer.
A: yes

sections.heading: Key Considerations & Policy Rules
sections.content_instructions:
List optionality, consent scope, storage, key rotation, and disablement scenarios as actionable rules.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Policy Rules:
  Rule 1: Suggested amounts must be derived only from the payer's own transaction history; no cross‑user data sharing is permitted.
  Rule 2: The maximum number of suggestions displayed shall not exceed three.
  Rule 3: Any suggestion displayed must be within the payer's daily transaction limit as defined by the PSP.
RULE: Stakeholder-friendly language only. No XML, no internal class names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required]** We assume the payer will be identified by the standard UPI Virtual Payment Address (VPA) – the same identifier used in the existing ReqPay message. Confirm, or specify a different identifier type (e.g., mobile number, Aadhaar‑linked ID).
A: yes proceed

Q: **[Required]** We assume the payee will also be identified by a VPA, matching the current payeeVPA field in the ReqPay request. Confirm, or indicate if an alternative identifier (e.g., merchant ID, phone number) is required.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** For the smart‑suggested‑amount flow we will apply the same per‑transaction ceiling as the baseline UPI rule – INR 15,000 – with mandatory PIN enforcement above that limit. Confirm, or provide a different maximum amount that should apply specifically to suggested‑amount payments.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** We will inherit the payer’s existing daily UPI aggregate limit set by the PSP (e.g., ₹1 lakh per day) for transactions generated via the suggestion UI. Confirm, or specify a distinct daily cap that should be enforced for this feature.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** To deliver the amount suggestions we propose adding two new API messages:  
   * **SuggestionRequest** – sent from the payer PSP to NPCI (or a designated suggestion service) with payerVPA and payeeVPA to retrieve historic amounts.  
   * **SuggestionResponse** – returns a list (up to three) of suggested amounts plus optional metadata (frequency, recency).  

   The existing ReqPay flow will remain unchanged after the user selects a suggestion. Confirm, or outline any additional request/response fields or alternative message names you prefer.
A: yes

sections.heading: Testing, Certification & Audits
sections.content_instructions:
Describe test environments, mandatory scenarios, certification steps, and audit expectations.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Test Scenarios:
  - Happy path – suggested amount selection | Verify that a user can select a suggested amount, edit it if needed, and complete payment successfully. | PSP Bank
  - PIN enforcement for amount >15,000 | Ensure that the UPI PIN screen appears when a suggested amount exceeds the 15,000 INR threshold. | PSP Bank
  - No suggestions available | Confirm that the app falls back to manual amount entry when no historical data exists for the payee. | PSP Bank
RULE: Stakeholder-friendly language only. No XML, no internal class names.

sections.heading: Annexure: Pre-Checks
sections.content_instructions:
Define pre-checks required before enrollment/enabling and at transaction time, with failure consequences for each. Derive the specific checks from the input.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS (derived from NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
User Journey (plain language — use in Product Construct):
  Step 1: User opens the UPI app and selects a contact or merchant.
  Step 2: PSP app fetches recent transaction amounts for that payee from NPCI and shows up to three suggested amounts.
  Step 3: User taps a suggested amount; the amount field is pre‑filled and remains editable.
  Step 4: If amount >15,000, the app prompts for UPI PIN; otherwise proceeds.
  Step 5: PSP sends ReqPay (with chosen amount) to NPCI.
  Step 6: NPCI performs authentication (ReqAuthDetail/RespAuthDetail) and processes debit/credit as per existing flow.
  Step 7: User receives transaction confirmation (RespTxnConfirmation).
Policy Rules (use in Key Considerations):
  Rule 1: Suggested amounts must be derived only from the payer's own transaction history; no cross‑user data sharing is permitted.
  Rule 2: The maximum number of suggestions displayed shall not exceed three.
  Rule 3: Any suggestion displayed must be within the payer's daily transaction limit as defined by the PSP.
Failure Scenarios (use in fallback sections):
  If PIN entry fails: transaction is declined with a Business Decline (BD) code and user is prompted to retry.
  If NPCI does not return authentication within the SLA: transaction is aborted and a Technical Decline (TD) is returned to the payer.
Testing Scenarios (use in Testing & Certification table):
  - Happy path – suggested amount selection | Verify that a user can select a suggested amount, edit it if needed, and complete payment successfully. | PSP Bank
  - PIN enforcement for amount >15,000 | Ensure that the UPI PIN screen appears when a suggested amount exceeds the 15,000 INR threshold. | PSP Bank
  - No suggestions available | Confirm that the app falls back to manual amount entry when no historical data exists for the payee. | PSP Bank
Dispute Framework:
  Standard UDIR dispute and chargeback processes apply unchanged; no new dispute types are introduced by the Smart Amount Suggestions feature.
RULE: Stakeholder-friendly language only. No XML, no XSD field names, no internal class names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required]** We assume the payer will be identified by the standard UPI Virtual Payment Address (VPA) – the same identifier used in the existing ReqPay message. Confirm, or specify a different identifier type (e.g., mobile number, Aadhaar‑linked ID).
A: yes proceed

Q: **[Required]** We assume the payee will also be identified by a VPA, matching the current payeeVPA field in the ReqPay request. Confirm, or indicate if an alternative identifier (e.g., merchant ID, phone number) is required.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** For the smart‑suggested‑amount flow we will apply the same per‑transaction ceiling as the baseline UPI rule – INR 15,000 – with mandatory PIN enforcement above that limit. Confirm, or provide a different maximum amount that should apply specifically to suggested‑amount payments.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** We will inherit the payer’s existing daily UPI aggregate limit set by the PSP (e.g., ₹1 lakh per day) for transactions generated via the suggestion UI. Confirm, or specify a distinct daily cap that should be enforced for this feature.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** To deliver the amount suggestions we propose adding two new API messages:  
   * **SuggestionRequest** – sent from the payer PSP to NPCI (or a designated suggestion service) with payerVPA and payeeVPA to retrieve historic amounts.  
   * **SuggestionResponse** – returns a list (up to three) of suggested amounts plus optional metadata (frequency, recency).  

   The existing ReqPay flow will remain unchanged after the user selects a suggestion. Confirm, or outline any additional request/response fields or alternative message names you prefer.
A: yes
sections.prompt_instruction: Split into Before Enrollment/Enabling and At Transaction Time. Each item should name the check, which participant performs it, and the consequence if it fails.
